title: iris pallida var. loppio
intro: Iris pallida var. loppio, also known as the Dalmatian iris, is a subspecies of the iris pallida plant, characterized by its vibrant purple-blue flowers and sword-shaped leaves. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been attributed to its flavonoid and sesquiterpene content. The health benefits of Iris pallida var. loppio include its potential to soothe skin irritations, reduce inflammation, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Iris pallida var. loppio is often prepared as a tincture, infusion, or salve, with some herbalists also utilizing it in aromatherapy blends to leverage its therapeutic benefits.
